content.problemStatement:
Implementation of QueueWe are implementing queue using an array, We first initialize an array with a fixed size which will be the max size of our queue. we will be also using three global variable here :-


front - for tracing the index of first element of queue.
rear - for tracing the index of rear element of queue.
maxSize - storing the max size of array.
current size - give the current number element in a queue
**Hint** : use currentsize variable to complete this function

isEmpty function
Checks if the queue is empty. Return True if isEmpty is Full else False.
isFull() Function:
Checks if the queue is full. Return True if Queue is Full else False.
size() Function:
Checks the current size of queue and return the current Size.
Task
Given three function for the implementation of queue
Complete the isempty,isfull and size  function.

Input Format
Input Format


First line: Integer front – index of the front element (-1 if empty).


Second line: Integer rear – index of the rear element (-1 if empty).


Third line: Integer maxSize – maximum capacity of the queue.



Output Format
Output Format:

Print whether the Queue is empty or not:

Queue is Empty   # if empty

Queue is Not Empty  # if not empty

Print whether the Queue is full or not:

Queue is Full   # if full

Queue is Not Full  # if not full

Print the current size of the queue:

Current Size: 0
constraints: Total input size fits in memory
content.inputFormat:
The input consists of a sequence of integers. Every three consecutive integers describe one test case:
1. front – index of the front element (‑1 if the queue is empty).
2. rear – index of the rear element (‑1 if the queue is empty).
3. maxSize – the maximum capacity of the queue.
There is no explicit number of test cases; read until end‑of‑file. Each test case is processed independently.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case output three lines:
1. "Queue is Empty" if the queue has no elements, otherwise "Queue is Not Empty".
2. "Queue is Full" if the queue contains maxSize elements, otherwise "Queue is Not Full".
3. "Current Size: X" where X is the number of elements currently stored in the queue.
If there are multiple test cases, the outputs of consecutive cases are printed one after another, each line terminated by a newline character.

sampleTestCases.output:
Queue is Empty
Queue is Not Full
Current Size: 0
sampleTestCases.explanation: Both front and rear are -1, therefore the queue contains no elements. Size = 0, so it is empty and not full.

sampleTestCases.output:
Queue is Not Empty
Queue is Full
Current Size: 5
sampleTestCases.explanation: front = 0, rear = 4, maxSize = 5. rear >= front, so size = 4‑0+1 = 5 which equals maxSize. Hence the queue is full and not empty.

sampleTestCases.output:
Queue is Not Empty
Queue is Not Full
Current Size: 1
sampleTestCases.explanation: Only one element is present (front == rear == 2). Size = 1, which is less than maxSize, so the queue is neither empty nor full.

hidden.label: minimum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput:
Queue is Empty
Queue is Not Full
Current Size: 0

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput:
Queue is Not Empty
Queue is Full
Current Size: 100000

hidden.label: edge case (wrap‑around full)
hidden.expectedOutput:
Queue is Not Empty
Queue is Full
Current Size: 10

hidden.label: general case 1
hidden.expectedOutput:
Queue is Not Empty
Queue is Not Full
Current Size: 6

hidden.label: general case 2
hidden.expectedOutput:
Queue is Not Empty
Queue is Not Full
Current Size: 5

solution.approach:
For each test case we first check whether the queue is empty: it is empty only when both front and rear are -1. If it is empty the current size is 0. Otherwise the size depends on the relative positions of front and rear in the circular array. If rear >= front the elements occupy a contiguous block and size = rear - front + 1. If rear < front the block wraps around the end of the array and size = maxSize - front + rear + 1. The queue is full when the computed size equals maxSize. The three required answers are then printed.
All operations are O(1) per test case, using only a few integer variables.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, overall O(T) where T is the number of test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the input storage (if any).
